data.markdown:
# **Access, affordability, education & advocacy.**

#### Our holistic approach to nutrition security is based on the belief that we should all be able to find, afford and learn about the foods that support our health and bring us joy.

## Healthy Food Access

Nutritious food should be easy to find in every neighborhood, from [**farmers markets**](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/farmers-markets) to [**corner stores**](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/corner-stores/) to [**supermarkets**](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/hffi/).

[Visit our Healthy Food Access Portal](http://healthyfoodaccess.org/)

![](https://thefoodtrust.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Access_green.png)

## Nutrition Incentives

Programs like [**Food Bucks** and **Food Bucks Rx**](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/food-bucks/) empower families, support farmers and strengthen local economies.

[Find Food Bucks coupons near you](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/food-bucks/)

![](https://thefoodtrust.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Affordability_green.png)

## Nutrition Education

The Food Trust promotes delicious, nutritious food choices through hands-on food education and cooking workshops, **[online](https://learning.thefoodtrust.org/)** and in-person through our **[Farm to School](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/farm-to-school/)** and **[Community Nutrition](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/community-nutrition/)** work.

[Visit our Online Learning Hub](https://learning.thefoodtrust.org/)

![](https://thefoodtrust.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Education_green.png)

## Policy & Advocacy

Together, through **institutional, local, state and federal policy change**, we can ensure that no one has to choose between eating healthy and eating enough.

[Learn more about our policy agenda](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/advocacy/)

![](https://thefoodtrust.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Advocacy_green.png)

* * *

## Our approach: Centering the community

Communities have the power to create a more just and nourishing food system. The Food Trust’s [asset-based](https://case.edu/socialwork/communityinnovation/training/asset-based-community-development) approach centers evidence-based programs, community engagement and policy advocacy. As we gather and listen to community voices, we know ideas will emerge. Through collaboration, we hope to provide a platform for those doing food-related work to come together to identify goals, needs and assets and to discuss ways that, by working together, we can find sustainable solutions to community health.

[Our evaluation method](https://thefoodtrust.org/what-we-do/evaluation/)

![](https://thefoodtrust.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/Untitled-design-3.png)

## Support our work

[Get Involved](https://thefoodtrust.org/get-involved/)
